subject: english
question: Choose the sentence that is grammatically correct.
options: A. She has been working here since five years.
B. She has been working here for five years.
C. She is working here since five years.
D. She works here since five years.
explanation: 'For' is used with a period/duration of time (five years), while 'since' is used with a specific point in time.

subject: english
question: Identify the grammatically correct sentence.
options: A. Neither of the two candidates were selected for the post.
B. Neither of the two candidates was selected for the post.
C. Neither of the two candidates have been selected for the post.
D. Neither of the two candidates are selected for the post.
explanation: 'Neither' is a singular pronoun and always takes a singular verb, so 'was' is correct.

subject: english
question:
Select the correctly rearranged sentence from the given jumbled parts:
P. to attend the meeting
Q. the district collector
R. has instructed all officers
S. scheduled for tomorrow
options: A. Q R P S
B. R Q P S
C. Q P R S
D. R P Q S
explanation: The logical order is 'The district collector has instructed all officers to attend the meeting scheduled for tomorrow' (Q-R-P-S).

subject: english
question: Choose the correctly corrected sentence: 'Each of the students have submitted their assignment.'
options: A. Each of the students has submitted their assignment.
B. Each of the students have submitted his assignment.
C. Each of the students has submitted his or her assignment.
D. Each of the students had submit their assignment.
explanation: 'Each' takes a singular verb 'has', and traditionally requires a singular pronoun 'his or her' for grammatical agreement.

subject: english
question: Which sentence is grammatically correct?
options: A. I am knowing him since childhood.
B. I know him since childhood.
C. I have known him since childhood.
D. I had known him since childhood.
explanation: 'Know' is a stative verb and, with 'since' indicating an action continuing from the past to the present, the present perfect tense 'have known' is correct.

subject: english
question:
Select the correctly ordered sentence from the jumbled words:
P. the Mantralaya building
Q. is located
R. in the heart of Mumbai
S. of Maharashtra
options: A. P S Q R
B. Q P S R
C. P Q S R
D. S P Q R
explanation: The correct sentence reads 'The Mantralaya building of Maharashtra is located in the heart of Mumbai' (P-S-Q-R).

subject: english
question: Identify the grammatically correct sentence.
options: A. He is one of the best officer in the department.
B. He is one of the best officers in the department.
C. He is one of the best officer of the department.
D. He is one of best officers in the department.
explanation: The phrase 'one of the' must be followed by a plural noun, so 'officers' is correct.

subject: english
question: Choose the correctly corrected version of: 'The number of unemployed youth are increasing every year.'
options: A. The number of unemployed youth is increasing every year.
B. The number of unemployed youth are increased every year.
C. A number of unemployed youth is increasing every year.
D. The numbers of unemployed youth is increasing every year.
explanation: 'The number of' takes a singular verb, whereas 'A number of' takes a plural verb; here 'the number of...is increasing' is correct.

subject: english
question:
Select the correctly rearranged sentence from the jumbled parts:
P. despite heavy rainfall
Q. the farmers of Vidarbha
R. continued sowing their crops
S. in the region
options: A. Q R P S
B. P Q R S
C. Q P R S
D. P R Q S
explanation: The logical sequence is 'The farmers of Vidarbha continued sowing their crops despite heavy rainfall in the region' (Q-R-P-S).

subject: english
question: Which of the following sentences contains no grammatical error?
options: A. She is senior than me in the office.
B. She is more senior than me in the office.
C. She is senior to me in the office.
D. She is more senior to me in the office.
explanation: The adjective 'senior' (a comparative of Latin origin) is followed by 'to', not 'than', so 'senior to me' is correct.

subject: english
question: Choose the grammatically correct sentence.
options: A. If I was you, I would apply for that post.
B. If I were you, I would apply for that post.
C. If I am you, I would apply for that post.
D. If I would be you, I would apply for that post.
explanation: In hypothetical/unreal conditional sentences (Type 2), the subjunctive 'were' is used for all subjects, including 'I'.

subject: english
question:
Select the correctly ordered sentence from the jumbled words:
P. every citizen
Q. before the election
R. must verify their names
S. in the voters' list
options: A. P R S Q
B. P Q R S
C. Q P R S
D. P R Q S
explanation: The correct sentence is 'Every citizen must verify their names in the voters' list before the election' (P-R-S-Q).
